title: Constraint conservation salt postalign-only isolation

queries: representation alignment token retention conservative transfer ablation
post alignment consistency structured transfer negative transfer ablation
task selective transfer auxiliary loss isolation graph to token alignment

evidence.why_now: The live blocker after exp210 and exp211 is no longer Amazon-side continuation but defining a truly differentiated salt-side Part 3 direction. The repeated post-implementation salt-side screens EXP206 and EXP209 kept the same active item-incoterms configuration, so exp212 in that unchanged form would only retune the already screened salt path. The next justified move is therefore a narrower causal isolation: keep Amazon on a Part1-like control under the current code, and test whether salt-side continuation survives when post-alignment token retention is kept but the coupled entity-identity and branch-orth penalties are removed.

hypothesis: If the useful salt-side Part 3 signal is mainly coming from preserving token-level basis targets after graph-to-token transfer, then keeping the validated item-incoterms transfer amplitudes plus sparse assignment and post-alignment retention, while removing the extra entity-identity and branch-orth penalties, should remain reference-positive on item-incoterms without needing to relaunch the unchanged exp206 or exp209 setting. If the signal collapses, then post-alignment retention alone is not the real carrying mechanism and later salt-side Optuna should not be spent on this narrower path.
notes: This is a screening-only salt-side follow-up after exp212 was stopped because the unchanged active salt-side path had already been screened repeatedly in EXP206 and EXP209.

The causal change is intentionally narrow: Amazon tasks stay on Part1-like controls under the current code, while item-incoterms keeps only post-alignment token retention on top of the validated transfer amplitudes.
Do not run Optuna or final full test from this candidate directly.
If item-incoterms loses its reference-positive continuation signal here, the next salt-side move should not be another near-duplicate postalign-only retune.

Scientific interpretation still follows the Stage 3 task-aware rule set: user-ltv subset/full-test scale mismatch remains active, and item-incoterms should still be judged scientifically against the stored full-test reference band rather than only the optimistic subset baseline.
